ConnectionStrings._comment: Local Postgres default (matches the Npgsql migrations under CoparentHub.Persistence/Migrations). Adjust to your local Postgres install/credentials.

_comment_security: Secrets (ConnectionStrings:DefaultConnection, Jwt:Secret, Encryption:Key) must NEVER be committed in a real project. A real Jwt:Secret was previously committed here; it has been removed and MUST be treated as compromised, rotate it before deploying. Leaving Jwt:Secret empty in Development is fine: Program.cs auto-generates a temporary secret on startup (tokens are invalidated whenever the API restarts). The Encryption:Key below IS checked in, but it is a well-known placeholder for local development only — never reuse it anywhere real data exists; see the comment on that key. See SECURITY.md.

Encryption._comment: DEV-ONLY placeholder key so a fresh clone runs immediately against a local, empty database. This value is public (it's in source control) and provides zero real confidentiality — never point this configuration at a database containing real user data, and always set a private, generated (`openssl rand -base64 32`) key via Encryption__Key in every shared/staging/production environment.

Seq._comment: Where structured logs (including per-request timing) are shipped. Defaults to localhost:5341, matching the seq service in docker-compose.yml — run it standalone with `docker run -e ACCEPT_EULA=Y -p 5341:80 datalust/seq` if you're running the API directly via `dotnet run` instead of docker compose.

Brevo._comment: Left empty for local dev — the app falls back to a no-op email sender and the invite-by-email feature just reports itself unavailable. Set your own free Brevo API key + verified sender email here (or via user-secrets) if you want to test real email delivery locally.

Vapid._comment: Left empty for local dev — the app falls back to a no-op push sender and the enable-notifications prompt just never appears. Generate your own free test key pair (VapidHelper.GenerateVapidKeys() or `npx web-push generate-vapid-keys`) here if you want to test real push delivery locally.

Admin._comment: Set to your own account's email if you want to test the announcement-broadcast endpoint locally.
